summary: Prostaglandin analogs used to treat glaucoma can cause black or brown discoloration due to altered pigmentation processes affecting both lash colorations and conjunctival appearance; other classes like beta blockers may have similar effects but aren't consistently reported compared to those associated specifically with prostaglandins based current medical literature findings. The primary focus remains on identifying which specific medication type is most commonly linked through empirical data supporting their causal relationship over alternatives considered here today
